LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM'S GENERAL DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF'S FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION UNDER A PLAIN READING OF GOVERNMENT CODE SECTION 21363.4. PLAINTIFF'S SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR EQUITABLE ESTOPPEL AGAINST A GOVERNMENT ENTITY. MEDINA VS. BOARD OF RETIREMENT. 112 C.A. 4TH, 864, 870. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO ALLEGE NEW FACTS THAT MAKE THIS COMPLAINT TIMELY. DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM'S GENERAL DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: Gregg McLean Adam, Esq. 743-2534 & Jennifer Stoughton, Esq. of Carroll, Burdick & McDonough LLP for pltfs & opposing parties; Harvey L. Leiderman, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP 659-5914 for deft & moving party CALPERS; Jake M. Hurley & Keith B. LaMar, State of California 916/324-0512 for deft Department of Personnel Administration.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff's first Cause of Action fails to state a Cause of Action Under a plain reading of Government Code Section 21363.4. Plaintiff's second Cause of Action fails to state a Cause of Action for equitable estoppel against a government entity. Medina vs. Board of Retirement. 112 C.A. 4th, 864, 870. Defendant to submit proposed form of order. (302/CWW/JW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5759

APPEARANCES: Gregg McLean Adam, Esq. 743-2534 & Jennifer Stoughton, Esq. of Carroll, Burdick & McDonough LLP for pltfs & opposing parties; Jake M. Hurley & Keith B. LaMar, State of California 916/324-0512 for deft & moving party Department of Personnel Administration; Harvey L. Leiderman, Esq. of Reed Smith LLP 659-5914 for deft CALPERS.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to amend the complaint to allege new facts that make this complaint timely. Plaintiff's claims are barred by the one year statute of Limintations. Government Code section 19815.8. Defendant to submit proposed form of order. (302/CWW/JW) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5759
